reasoning: The 2025 National Heads-Up Poker Championship is a 64-player invitation event that was filmed between August 1 and August 3, 2025. The tournament results have been kept secret due to NDAs because the event broadcasts on Peacock in the fall of 2025. However, in mid-October 2025, prediction markets (namely Polymarket) exhibited significant activity indicating a major insider leak. Sam Soverel's odds of winning spiked to over 90-99% on Polymarket, largely spoiling that Soverel is the winner of the tournament. While John Smith was a participant in the tournament (having drawn Justin Gavri in the first round), there is no evidence indicating he won, and the overwhelming consensus from the pre-taped leak is that Sam Soverel won. Therefore, the probability that John Smith won the tournament is effectively zero.
